_meta.description: Evidence-graded summary of common nutritional supplements for metabolic health and weight management

grades.A: Multiple independent high-quality RCTs with consistent findings OR established systematic review/meta-analysis
grades.B: Single well-designed RCT OR consistent prospective observational data
grades.C: Mechanistic evidence, animal studies only, or conflicting human data
grades.D: No credible human evidence, or contradicted by higher-quality studies

supplements.name: Creatine Monohydrate
supplements.primary_claim: Increased strength output, lean muscle mass support
supplements.timing: Timing not critical; post-workout has marginal benefit
supplements.key_citation: Lanhers C et al. Creatine supplementation and upper limb strength performance. Sports Med. 2017.
supplements.safety: Well-established safety profile; adequate hydration important
supplements.taiwan_note: Available at most 藥局 and health food stores; look for GMP-certified products

supplements.name: Caffeine
supplements.timing: 60 minutes before exercise or cognitive task
supplements.key_citation: Guest NS et al. International society of sports nutrition position stand: caffeine and exercise performance. JISSN. 2021.
supplements.safety: Tolerance develops; cycling recommended (2 weeks on, 1 week off). Avoid >400mg/day.
supplements.taiwan_note: Average cup of 台灣咖啡 (hand-drip) contains 80–120mg caffeine

supplements.name: Omega-3 Fatty Acids (EPA/DHA)
supplements.effective_dose: 1–2g EPA+DHA/day for general health; 4g/day for hypertriglyceridemia (prescription level)
supplements.timing: With meals to reduce GI discomfort and improve absorption
supplements.key_citation: AHA Scientific Advisory on Omega-3 Fatty Acids and Cardiovascular Disease. Circulation. 2018.
supplements.safety: High doses (>3g/day) may increase bleeding time; consult physician if on anticoagulants
supplements.selection_note: Look for products with combined EPA+DHA ≥500mg per capsule; IFOS or similar third-party certification preferred

supplements.name: Probiotics
supplements.primary_claim: Gut microbiome support, digestive health, immune function
supplements.notes: Effects are strain-specific. Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium species are most studied. Evidence for weight loss is weak (C); digestive benefits are stronger (B).
supplements.effective_dose: 1–10 billion CFU/day; specific to strain and indication
supplements.timing: Best taken before meals or as directed on product
supplements.key_citation: Hill C et al. Expert consensus document. The International Scientific Association for Probiotics and Prebiotics consensus statement. Nat Rev Gastroenterol Hepatol. 2014.
supplements.safety: Generally safe; immunocompromised individuals should consult physician
supplements.taiwan_note: Taiwan probiotics market is heavily regulated under 健康食品認證; look for 衛福部 health food certification

supplements.name: Vitamin C (Ascorbic Acid)
supplements.timing: Can be taken any time; split doses improve absorption above 200mg
supplements.key_citation: Carr AC, Maggini S. Vitamin C and Immune Function. Nutrients. 2017.
supplements.safety: High doses (>1g/day) may cause GI discomfort; kidney stone risk in susceptible individuals

supplements.name: Lutein + Zeaxanthin
supplements.key_citation: AREDS2 Research Group. Lutein + zeaxanthin and omega-3 fatty acids for age-related macular degeneration. JAMA. 2013.
supplements.notes: Evidence is specifically for slowing AMD progression in those already diagnosed; prevention benefit in healthy eyes is B-grade
food_sources: egg yolk 蛋黃 (most bioavailable form)
supplements.taiwan_note: Very popular supplement in Taiwan; most products use FloraGLO or Kemin-sourced lutein — these brands have the most clinical data

supplements.name: Vitamin D
supplements.primary_claim: Bone health, immune function, calcium absorption
supplements.effective_dose: Taiwan DRI: 400–600 IU/day (adults); deficiency treatment: 1500–2000 IU/day
supplements.testing_note: Serum 25(OH)D target: 30–50 ng/mL. Supplement if <20 ng/mL.
supplements.key_citation: Holick MF. Vitamin D deficiency. NEJM. 2007.
supplements.taiwan_note: Despite abundant sunlight, indoor lifestyles mean vitamin D deficiency is common in Taiwan urban populations

supplements.name: B-Complex Vitamins
supplements.notes: B vitamins are essential cofactors in metabolic pathways. Supplementation benefits are clear in deficiency states but limited in well-nourished individuals. B12 deficiency is common in vegans/vegetarians.
supplements.effective_dose: Per Taiwan DRIs; most supplements provide far above DRI — bioavailability limits toxicity risk
supplements.timing: Morning preferred (B vitamins may cause alertness/vivid dreams if taken at night)
supplements.key_citation: Kennedy DO. B Vitamins and the Brain: Mechanisms, Dose and Efficacy. Nutrients. 2016.

supplements.name: Green Tea Extract (EGCG)
supplements.notes: Meta-analyses show small but statistically significant effects on fat oxidation (-0.2 to -0.5 kg over 12 weeks). Not clinically meaningful for most individuals. Beneficial as a food (green tea) rather than supplement.
supplements.key_citation: Hursel R et al. The effects of green tea on weight loss and weight maintenance. Obes Rev. 2009.
supplements.safety: High-dose EGCG supplements (>800mg/day) associated with hepatotoxicity risk; stick to beverage form

supplements.name: CLA (Conjugated Linoleic Acid)
supplements.notes: Modest effect in meta-analyses (~0.1 kg/week reduction vs placebo); effect size not clinically meaningful for most. May negatively affect lipid profiles with long-term use.
supplements.key_citation: Whigham LD et al. Efficacy of conjugated linoleic acid for reducing fat mass. Am J Clin Nutr. 2007.

supplements.name: Garcinia Cambogia (HCA)
supplements.notes: A 1998 JAMA double-blind RCT showed no significant weight loss vs placebo. Subsequent meta-analyses confirm small effects indistinguishable from statistical noise. Not recommended.
supplements.key_citation: Heymsfield SB et al. Garcinia cambogia (Hydroxycitric Acid) as a Potential Antiobesity Agent. JAMA. 1998.
